content: Monosodium glutamate (MSG) is the sodium salt of glutamic acid, an amino acid that our bodies can produce.
A carboxyl group in glutamic acid becomes negatively charged when it loses its hydrogen atom and can bond with a positively charged ion, forming MSG.
There is a widely held belief that MSG is harmful, despite its structure being similar to naturally occurring glutamic acid in the body.
The scientific method was observed in the context of MSG, confirming or refuting a hypothesis about its safety without empirical evidence.
Study replicability is crucial for accepting conclusions, as conclusions can change based on new studies and research.
